title: fab doctor --fix — one-time marker migration (fabric:knowledge-base → fabric:bootstrap)
description: Add a `bootstrap_marker_migration_required` fixable_error to doctor. Detection scans CLAUDE.md / root AGENTS.md / .cursor/rules (legacy path) and .cursor/rules/fabric-bootstrap.mdc for the LEGACY_KB_REGEX. Fix path rewrites the legacy marker to the new fabric:bootstrap marker in place AND emits a bootstrap_marker_migrated ledger event. Mirrors the existing fixMcpConfigInWrongFile pattern (read → atomicWriteText → append ledger event). Runs FIRST in runDoctorFix dispatcher so subsequent L1/L2 drift inspections see post-rename state. Idempotent: post-migration scan finds nothing to do.

files.change: Add new event variant: `z.object({ event_type: z.literal('bootstrap_marker_migrated'), path: z.string(), migrated_count: z.number().int().nonnegative(), legacy_marker: z.literal('fabric:knowledge-base'), new_marker: z.literal('fabric:bootstrap'), timestamp: z.string() })`. Add to the discriminated union.

files.target: inspect helper (new) + createCheck (new) — near L1779 inspectBootstrapAnchor neighborhood
files.change: Add `inspectBootstrapMarkerMigration(target): Promise<{ filesNeedingMigration: string[] }>` — scans CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md / .cursor/rules / .cursor/rules/fabric-bootstrap.mdc for LEGACY_KB_REGEX matches; returns paths where matches found. Add `createBootstrapMarkerMigrationCheck(inspection): DoctorCheck` — returns issueCheck(name='bootstrap-marker-migration', kind='fixable_error', code='bootstrap_marker_migration_required', actionHint='Run `fab doctor --fix` to migrate to fabric:bootstrap marker') when filesNeedingMigration is non-empty, else okCheck.

Append `checks.push(createBootstrapMarkerMigrationCheck(await inspectBootstrapMarkerMigration(target)))` to the checks aggregation. Inspection runs in the Promise.all block at L708-L718 to keep performance parity.

files.target: runDoctorFix dispatcher (L893-L987) — FIRST if-block, before reconcileKnowledge
files.change: Insert new if-block: `if (before.fixable_errors.some(i => i.code === 'bootstrap_marker_migration_required')) { const migrated = await migrateBootstrapMarkers(projectRoot); fixed.push(findIssue(before, 'bootstrap_marker_migration_required')!); for (const path of migrated.paths) { await appendEventLedgerEvent(projectRoot, { event_type: 'bootstrap_marker_migrated', path, migrated_count: migrated.countPerPath[path] ?? 1, legacy_marker: 'fabric:knowledge-base', new_marker: 'fabric:bootstrap', timestamp: new Date().toISOString() }).catch(() => {}); } }`. Position FIRST so L1/L2 drift checks (TASK-05) see new markers.

Add `async function migrateBootstrapMarkers(projectRoot: string): Promise<{ paths: string[]; countPerPath: Record<string, number> }>` — iterates [CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, .cursor/rules, .cursor/rules/fabric-bootstrap.mdc], reads each if exists, applies `.replace(/<!-- fabric:knowledge-base:begin -->/g, '<!-- fabric:bootstrap:begin -->').replace(/<!-- fabric:knowledge-base:end -->/g, '<!-- fabric:bootstrap:end -->')`, atomicWriteText if differs, records path + count of replacements. Idempotent: re-running on already-migrated file is a no-op (replace returns same string).

implementation: Extend eventLedgerEventSchema with bootstrap_marker_migrated literal variant
Add inspectBootstrapMarkerMigration function to doctor.ts — scans 4 target paths for LEGACY_KB_REGEX
Add createBootstrapMarkerMigrationCheck — issueCheck fixable_error when files-needing-migration non-empty
Append the check to runDoctorReport's checks[] aggregation in the L784-L857 block
Add the runDoctorFix dispatcher if-block AT THE TOP of the dispatcher (before reconcileKnowledge) so subsequent L1/L2 inspections see post-migration state
Add migrateBootstrapMarkers helper — read/replace/atomicWriteText per path; emit bootstrap_marker_migrated event per migrated file (best-effort)
Verify idempotency: post-fix re-run of inspectBootstrapMarkerMigration returns empty filesNeedingMigration

reference.pattern: fixMcpConfigInWrongFile at doctor.ts:4395-4435 — gold-standard one-time migration that rewrites a file via atomicWriteText and emits mcp_config_migrated ledger event
reference.examples: fixMcpConfigInWrongFile shows: read → mutate → atomicWriteText → emit typed event. Use appendEventLedgerEvent best-effort (silent try/catch). Idempotency: re-run finds no further work.

criteria: eventLedgerEventSchema accepts new variant `{ event_type: 'bootstrap_marker_migrated', path, migrated_count, legacy_marker, new_marker, timestamp }`
Fixture with legacy fabric:knowledge-base markers in AGENTS.md → runDoctorReport returns status='error' with `bootstrap_marker_migration_required` code in fixable_errors[]

After runDoctorFix on the legacy fixture: file contains zero fabric:knowledge-base marker substrings (verified via .match(/fabric:knowledge-base/g) === null) AND contains exactly one fabric:bootstrap:begin + one fabric:bootstrap:end pair
events.jsonl contains at least one `bootstrap_marker_migrated` event after fix
Re-running runDoctorReport on the post-fix state returns status='ok' (no `bootstrap_marker_migration_required` in fixable_errors)
Re-running runDoctorFix on the post-fix state is a no-op (zero files rewritten, zero new ledger events)
Marker migration runs BEFORE reconcileKnowledge in the dispatcher (verify by reading runDoctorFix top-to-bottom)
Migration covers all four target paths: CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, .cursor/rules (legacy path), .cursor/rules/fabric-bootstrap.mdc

rationale.chosen_approach: Separate fixable_error code 'bootstrap_marker_migration_required' (clarification option 1 from doctor-flow exploration). Gives operators an explicit, granular audit trail with one ledger event per migrated file. Runs FIRST in dispatcher so L1/L2 drift checks see post-rename state.
alternatives_considered: Bundle into L2 drift fix (fewer codes but loses audit granularity)
Run automatically on every --fix invocation as idempotent no-op (no audit trail)
rationale.tradeoffs: Extra check + ledger event variant vs zero new surfaces. Acceptable; rc.19 spec explicitly calls out marker rename as migration concern.

manual_checks: Seed a fixture with legacy markers in AGENTS.md, run `fab doctor`, verify exit=1 with fixable_error
Run `fab doctor --fix`, verify markers rewritten and exit=0
success_metrics: Zero LEGACY_KB_REGEX matches after fix across all four target paths
Exactly one bootstrap_marker_migrated event per migrated file in events.jsonl
Idempotent: second --fix produces no new events
integration: Covered by TASK-09 service-layer migration test

risks.description: User has edited content inside legacy markers — marker rename preserves their edits (managed-section convention says inside-marker content is fabric-owned, but during migration we should preserve to be safe)
risks.mitigation: Marker migration is a pure marker token rewrite — body content is untouched. After migration, the next runDoctorReport may flag L2 drift (TASK-05) which is the proper signal for body-content drift.

risks.description: Ledger event append fails on disk full / permission error — should not roll back the file rewrite (best-effort)
risks.mitigation: Wrap appendEventLedgerEvent in try/catch with silent failure (matches existing event-emission pattern)

key_functions.purpose: Scan four target paths for legacy fabric:knowledge-base marker substring
key_functions.returns: { filesNeedingMigration: string[] } where each entry is an absolute path

key_functions.purpose: Build the DoctorCheck — fixable_error when filesNeedingMigration non-empty, ok otherwise

key_functions.signature: migrateBootstrapMarkers(projectRoot: string): Promise<{ paths: string[]; countPerPath: Record<string, number> }>
key_functions.purpose: Rewrite legacy marker tokens to new marker tokens across four target paths; idempotent
